title: Ankle / foot pain

red_flags.text: Identifies severe pain or swelling, discoloration, weakness, movement loss, numbness, coldness, deformity, persistent symptoms, fracture or dislocation concern, and clinician-selected radiography, ultrasound, or MRI.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped neurovascular, deformity, fever, hot-joint, movement, and urgent concern fields.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Identifies severe pain or swelling, discoloration, weakness, movement loss, numbness, coldness, deformity, persistent symptoms, fracture or dislocation concern, and clinician-selected radiography, ultrasound, or MRI.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Muscle Sprains & Strains – 47

red_flags.text: Supports clinician-documented RICE, activity protection, mobilization, analgesia, physical therapy, return to activity, one-week review, worsening review, and urgent escalation for severe, neurovascular, deformity, weight-bearing, fever, or hot-joint concerns.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-documented RICE, activity, analgesia, physiotherapy, return, and follow-up fields.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-documented RICE, activity protection, mobilization, analgesia, physical therapy, return to activity, one-week review, worsening review, and urgent escalation for severe, neurovascular, deformity, weight-bearing, fever, or hot-joint concerns.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Muscle Sprains & Strains – 47

examination.text: Supports pain, swelling, bruising, restricted movement, weight-bearing difficulty, instability, loss of function, mechanism, timing, force, direction, pop or snap, aggravating conditions, and previous episodes.
examination.rationale: Supports mapped ankle-injury mechanism, symptom, function, examination, and recurrence fields in sprain-compatible presentations.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports pain, swelling, bruising, restricted movement, weight-bearing difficulty, instability, loss of function, mechanism, timing, force, direction, pop or snap, aggravating conditions, and previous episodes.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Muscle Sprains & Strains – 47

investigations.text: Covers non-complex fracture assessment and follow-up, repeated pain assessment, definitive X-ray reporting, Ottawa ankle and foot rules, and selected scaphoid imaging.
investigations.rationale: Supports selected clinician-entered ankle or foot X-ray review without importing unstated Ottawa-rule criteria.
investigations.evidence_extract: Covers non-complex fracture assessment and follow-up, repeated pain assessment, definitive X-ray reporting, Ottawa ankle and foot rules, and selected scaphoid imaging.

follow_up.text: Defines sprains and strains, common ankle, knee, wrist and muscle contexts, Dubai telehealth scope, injury mechanisms, athletic activity, overexertion, repetitive motion, and recurrence risks.
follow_up.rationale: Reviewed to establish that the DHA document applies to sprain and strain presentations; no additional broad ankle or foot legacy items were mapped from scope alone.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Defines sprains and strains, common ankle, knee, wrist and muscle contexts, Dubai telehealth scope, injury mechanisms, athletic activity, overexertion, repetitive motion, and recurrence risks.

source_limitations: Plantar fasciitis, heel spur, locking, open wound, anterior drawer, talar tilt, exact Ottawa-rule components, fixed 24-hour or 1–2-week timing, and broad foot-pain causes remain unsupported.
The combined sprain or plantar-fasciitis diagnosis label remains clinician-stated.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
